parts.text:
The error is clear: a `<button>` element is nested inside another `<button>` element, which is invalid HTML. The stack trace shows the issue is in `src/components/data-table-filter/components/filter-selector.tsx:141-142`.

The `PopoverTrigger` with `asChild={true}` is rendering a native `<button>` (from Base UI), and inside it, the `<Button>` component (shadcn) also renders a native `<button>`. This creates a button-within-button situation.

Let me look at the file to understand the exact code.

parts.text:
Now I can see the issue. At line 141, `PopoverTrigger` with `asChild={true}` expects its child to be the trigger element. However, the child is a `<Button>` component which renders a native `<button>`. The `PopoverTrigger` itself also renders a `<button>` when `asChild` is not working as expected.

Wait, actually the issue is: `PopoverTrigger` with `asChild={true}` should merge its props onto the child element instead of rendering its own `<button>`. But looking at the error stack, it seems like BOTH a button is rendered by PopoverTrigger AND a button is rendered by the `<Button>` component.

Let me check the PopoverTrigger component to understand how it works.

Actually, looking more carefully at the error stack:

```
<PopoverTrigger asChild={true}>
  <button ... data-base-ui-click-trigger="" ... data-slot="popover-trigger" asChild={true}>
    <Button className="h-7" variant="outline">
      <Button className={"group/bu..."} data-slot="button">
        <button ... data-slot="button" ...>
```

It seems like the `PopoverTrigger` is still rendering its own `<button>` even with `asChild={true}`. This suggests the Popover component is from Base UI (not Radix), and it handles `asChild` differently.

Wait, let me check the popover component to understand the implementation.

parts.text: Now I understand the issue clearly: 1. `PopoverTrigger` is from `@base-ui/react/popover` - it renders a native ` ``` to: ```tsx {!hasFilters && {t("filter", locale)}} ``` 2. Import `buttonVariants` from `@/components/ui/button` 3. Remove `Button` import if it's no longer used in this file Let me check if `Button` is used elsewhere in this file... Looking at the code, `Button` is only used at line 142. So we can remove the import. Actually wait, let me also check if there are other similar patterns in the codebase where Button is nested inside PopoverTrigger. Let me also double-check: does the `PopoverTrigger` from Base UI support children? Yes, it does - it renders a `

The issue is at `src/components/data-table-filter/components/filter-selector.tsx:141-148`. Both `PopoverTrigger` (Base UI) and `Button` (Base UI) render native ` ``` to: ```tsx {!hasFilters && {t("filter", locale)}} ``` This eliminates the nested `
The user wants me to proceed with the fix. Let me make the edits to the filter-selector.tsx file.

state.title: Run typecheck on project

parts.text:
No new type errors from my change. The existing errors are unrelated.

Fixed: removed the nested `<button>` by replacing `<PopoverTrigger asChild><Button>` with a single `<PopoverTrigger>` that has `buttonVariants` styling applied directly. The `PopoverTrigger` (Base UI) renders its own `<button>`, so using `asChild` with another `<Button>` (which also renders a `<button>`) created the invalid nested button.
